quickAnswer: To handle routing new leads and follow-up tasks without manual copying, Start with lead forms, decide that the output must be simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow, use Zapier AI for one reviewable version, and check it before changing formats or adding another tool. Zapier AI should create the first reviewable version, Make belongs in the second pass only when simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow needs more control, and n8n should be saved for review or export after the core result is right.
quickDecision: For routing new leads and follow-up tasks without manual copying, choose Zapier AI first when the input is lead forms and the output needs to be simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow. Choose Make when routing matters more. Choose n8n when you need a lighter review, export, or fallback pass.

steps.title: Prepare lead forms
steps.detail: Put the real input in one place before opening a tool: lead forms, customer questions, appointment requests, task lists, routing new leads and follow-up tasks without manual copying, any required facts, the audience, and the format you need at the end. Remove private details you are not allowed to share.
steps.why: AI output is only as useful as the material and constraints you give it.
steps.output: A clean input bundle and one clear target format: simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow.

steps.title: Ask for the first usable version
steps.detail: Use Zapier AI for a first pass that tries to routing new leads and follow-up tasks without manual copying. Ask it to preserve source facts, flag uncertainty, and organize the result into sections you can review.
steps.why: The first pass should create a reviewable simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow, not a finished answer you trust blindly.
steps.output: A structured simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow with visible gaps or uncertain points.

steps.title: Check the answer before polishing
steps.detail: Compare the AI output with lead forms. Fix names, dates, numbers, unsupported claims, missing context, and tone before you ask for nicer wording.
steps.why: Polished wrong information is harder to catch than rough information.
steps.output: A corrected simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow that is ready for formatting or wording cleanup.

steps.title: Change the format only after the facts are right
steps.detail: Use Make when the verified simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow needs a different format, structure, or source-aware second pass. Keep the same facts from lead forms so the rewrite does not drift from the original.
steps.why: Format changes are safer after the substance is already checked.
steps.output: A version that fits the channel, document, post, or recipient.

steps.title: Finish with a human review
steps.detail: Use n8n for cleanup only after the main simple automated intake, routing, reminders, or follow-up workflow is checked against lead forms. Read the final result as the recipient would: on the device, in the app, or in the document where it will actually be used.
steps.why: The final review catches tone, missing context, and practical usability issues.
steps.output: A final result you can send, post, study from, or save with confidence.

bestPicksBySituation.situation: If you already have lead forms, choose Zapier AI.
